arts.ttl: Reading captain available for League One return at Wigan Athletic after 10 months out
arts.sum: Reading captain Andy Yiadom is available to make his long-awaited first-team return tomorrow. The 33-year-old has not featured since April after suffering injury during a 2-0 win at Bristol Rovers. Since then, the club captain has worked his way back and featured in the last three Under-21 matches, incrementally increasing his load until lasting the full 90 minutes in Monday's 3-0 defeat to West Ham United. The Royals sit ninth in League One with 14 matches remaining, only three points outside the play-offs ahead of the trip to Lancashire. arts.sum: In the second of a two-part special, our panel of Latics experts run the rule over a hugely positive week, with Saturday’s goalless draw at second-placed Wycombe being followed by a midweek home win over fifth-placed Huddersfield...

arts.ttl: Reading boss holding nerve on 'pushing the button' as free agent option remains open
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt has confirmed that they have looked at up to six names as they weigh up bringing in an extra player for the League One run-in. In the winter window, the Royals lost Dean Bouzanis as his contract was mutually terminated and therefore one space is available to fill. With 14 matches remaining, ahead of tomorrow's trip to Wigan Athletic, the Royals are only three points outside the play-offs in ninth. Hunt has confirmed that the EFL have given the club the green light to bring in a new face, albeit under strict restrictions, and now it comes down to when they 'push the button.'

arts.ttl: Reading boss addresses concerning contract situation and EFL regulation 'hurdle'
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt has confirmed that 'conversations' have taken place regarding contract extensions for next season, but nothing can be done until the 'hurdle' of EFL embargo is cleared. The Royals are sat ninth heading into the final 14 League One matches of the season, just three points outside the play-offs. However, many supporters are concerned about the prospect of next year, with only five players currently registered for the campaign starting in August. While Hunt shares those concerns, the former Championship winner has confirmed there is little the club can do currently as an EFL embargo is in place.

arts.ttl: Returning Wigan Athletic star has a point to prove
arts.sum: Dion Rankine is desperate to make up for lost time - and help Wigan Athletic propel up the League One table between now and the end of the season.

arts.ttl: 'Nothing changes for us': Reading boss addresses takeover talk amid exclusivity deal
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt has admitted that some of his players are walking around with bigger smiles on their faces than usual following the news that the club are in exclusive talks with a new potential owner. Up for sale for over 18 months, the Royals have battled against the odds to remain in with a chance of promotion under Dai Yongge, currently just three points outside the play-offs heading into a trip to Wigan Athletic on Saturday. On Monday, the club confirmed that exclusive talks were underway over a 'quick' end to negotiations, with the Reading Chronicle revealing that this was American investor Robert Platek.
